title: AI Impact Dimensions on Talent Demands
description: Framework for examining how AI implementation changes organizational talent demands across 3,003 KSAOs

1.name: Replaced by AI
1.description: Routine cognitive tasks, data entry, basic analysis, and rule-based operations are most vulnerable to full AI automation.
1.hypothesis: Occupations with high concentration of these KSAOs will see declining demand for these specific skills.

2.name: Augmented by AI
2.description: AI enhances but does not replace human expertise. Workers become more productive with AI assistance.
2.hypothesis: Increasing demand for humans who can effectively leverage AI tools while providing uniquely human judgment.

3.name: Building & Managing AI
3.full_name: Skills for BUILDING & MANAGING AI (AI Technology Skills)
3.description: Direct AI tool creation and management. Rapidly growing demand.
3.hypothesis: These categories show the strongest demand growth as organizations build AI capabilities.

4.name: Resistant to AI
4.description: Human-centric capabilities that AI cannot replicate.
4.hypothesis: Stable or increasing demand as these become more differentiating in an AI-augmented workplace.

5.name: Transformed by AI
5.description: The fundamental human task remains but workflows are restructured around AI capabilities.
5.hypothesis: Workers must adapt HOW they use these skills, even as core competency remains relevant.

1.name: Cognitive Abilities
1.ai_impact_description: Core target for AI augmentation/replacement. Routine cognitive tasks (pattern matching, calculation, comprehension) are increasingly performed by AI.

2.name: Interpersonal & Social Skills
2.ai_impact_description: Most resistant to AI replacement. Emotional intelligence, persuasion, negotiation, and social perceptiveness are distinctly human.

3.name: Higher-Order Thinking & Problem Solving
3.ai_impact_description: Partially augmented by AI. AI assists with information synthesis and analysis, but human judgment, critical evaluation of AI outputs, and creative problem-solving remain essential.

4.name: Management & Leadership
4.ai_impact_description: Transformed, not replaced. Leaders must now manage AI-augmented teams, allocate AI resources, interpret AI-driven insights for strategy.

5.name: Physical & Psychomotor Abilities
5.ai_impact_description: Not directly affected by AI software, but relevant to robotics/physical automation research.

6.name: Sensory & Perceptual Abilities
6.ai_impact_description: Minimal direct AI impact. Computer vision and speech recognition create AI analogues, but human sensory abilities remain distinct.

7.name: Domain-Specific Knowledge
7.ai_impact_description: AI changes how domain knowledge is accessed and applied. Experts shift from memorization to curation, validation, and application of AI-retrieved information.

8.name: Technical Equipment & Maintenance Skills
8.ai_impact_description: Partially automatable via IoT, predictive maintenance AI, and smart diagnostics. Technicians increasingly work alongside AI systems.

9.name: AI, Machine Learning & Advanced Analytics
9.ai_impact_description: Directly represents AI capabilities - these are the tools CREATING disruption. Demand for AI/ML skills is exploding.

10.name: Data Management, Analytics & Business Intelligence
10.ai_impact_description: Heavily augmented by AI (natural language querying, auto-generated reports, anomaly detection). Traditional BI skills face disruption.

11.name: Enterprise & Business Operations Software
11.ai_impact_description: AI being embedded into ERP/CRM/accounting/logistics systems. Workers shift from data entry to exception handling and AI oversight.

12.name: Communication, Collaboration & Productivity Tools
12.ai_impact_description: AI augments communication (smart compose, auto-summarization, real-time translation). Core communication skills remain human.

13.name: Design, Creative & Multimedia Tools
13.ai_impact_description: Generative AI (image, video, text, code) is rapidly transforming creative workflows. Human creativity directs AI capabilities.

14.name: Engineering, CAD/CAM & Scientific Software
14.ai_impact_description: AI enhances generative design, automated simulation, predictive testing. Engineers leverage AI for optimization.

15.name: IT Infrastructure, Networking & Cybersecurity
15.ai_impact_description: AI transforms security (threat detection, anomaly detection) and IT operations (AIOps, predictive infrastructure management).

16.name: Healthcare & Life Sciences Technology
16.ai_impact_description: AI transforming diagnostics, clinical decision support, drug discovery, and administrative workflows in healthcare.

17.name: HR, Workforce Management & Training Technology
17.ai_impact_description: AI automates resume screening, skills matching, performance analytics, and learning personalization.

18.name: General-Purpose & Industry-Specific Software
18.ai_impact_description: Vendor-specific software products spanning diverse industries. AI being integrated across all categories.
